outlets.name: CBS News
outlets.headline: Iran's Pezeshkian says 'dialogue is not surrender' as Pakistan mediation continues on Day 83
outlets.summary: Day 83 opens with Iran insisting it won't capitulate — Pezeshkian declared 'all diplomatic paths remain open' while insisting 'we will never bow our heads.' Pakistan's Interior Minister Naqvi is in Tehran for two-day face-to-face mediation as message exchanges with the US continue via Islamabad.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump and Netanyahu diverge on Iran war's future in tense phone call
outlets.summary: CNN reports Netanyahu pushed to resume the war to further degrade Iran's military, while Trump pursued a diplomatic framework. Sources described Netanyahu as 'highly skeptical' of negotiations; his 'hair was on fire' during the call.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Iran PGSA declares 'controlled maritime zone' at Hormuz — requiring all vessels to register and pay
outlets.summary: Iran's Persian Gulf Strait Authority formalized its Hormuz control regime, requiring all transiting vessels to register cargo, crew, and ownership — and pay up to $2M for authorization permits. Maritime law experts say it violates UNCLOS but Iran is positioning itself as a legitimate transit authority.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: IRGC threatens war will spread 'far beyond the region' — repositioning military assets as Trump deadline hits
outlets.summary: Fox's Day 82 liveblog covers the IRGC's formal threat to extend war 'far beyond the region,' acknowledgment of repositioning military assets abroad, and Khamenei's 'Third Sacred Defense' jihad framing — painting Iran as making zero concessions while Trump holds back.

outlets.name: Breitbart
outlets.headline: Iran war 'not a forever war,' Vance says — but warns of 'kinetic option' if deal fails
outlets.summary: Breitbart and FNC Gen. Keane: 'We're six weeks in to a two-week deadline for an Iran deal — seems like there's just delay.' The right-flank frustration with protracted diplomacy is growing as Trump's original 'very fast' war timeline keeps extending.

outlets.name: Rubio / The Hill
outlets.headline: Rubio says Iran's 'insane in the brain' leaders should not test Trump as Hormuz tensions rise
outlets.summary: Secretary of State Rubio's sharp warning signals the administration is maintaining maximum pressure rhetoric even as Trump pauses military action. The 'insane in the brain' characterization is designed to deter Iran from misreading diplomatic engagement as weakness.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war Day 83: Iran PGSA declares 'controlled maritime zone'; IRGC threatens war beyond region; Pakistan mediating
outlets.summary: Day 83 opens with Iran formalizing Hormuz control under the PGSA 'controlled maritime zone' framework, IRGC threatening war 'far beyond the region,' and Pakistan FM in Tehran mediating US-Iran message exchange. Iran reviewing latest US proposals. 1,550+ commercial vessels remain stranded.

outlets.name: CNBC
outlets.headline: Iran threatens to extend conflict 'beyond the region' if US and Israel resume attacks
outlets.summary: IRGC formal statement warns of 'devastating blows' if attacks resume, with confirmed repositioning of military assets abroad. CNBC analysis: the 2-3 day window set by Gulf states is a delay, not a resolution — underlying conditions for resumed conflict remain unchanged.

outlets.name: Xinhua
outlets.headline: Iran says reviewing US views as exchange of messages continues through Pakistan
outlets.summary: Iran's FM spokesman Baghaei confirms US-Iran message exchange continues through Pakistan mediator. Pakistan Interior Minister Naqvi in Tehran for two-day mediation meeting Pezeshkian, Momeni, and Ghalibaf. Iran demands frozen asset release and end to US 'maritime piracy.'

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: IRGC threatens war will spread far beyond the region if US renews attacks on Iran
outlets.summary: The IRGC's formal 'war beyond the region' threat is being analyzed by Israeli security officials as a credible signal — IRGC Quds Force has pre-positioned assets in 40+ countries. Times of Israel notes majority of Israelis believe ending the war now contradicts their security interests.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: 'Bibi's hair was on fire': Inside the tense Trump-Netanyahu call over Iran peace framework
outlets.summary: Trump and Netanyahu clashed sharply over whether to pursue a 30-day letter of intent or resume strikes to destroy Iran's infrastructure. The US-Israel split is the most serious since the war began — if Israel acts unilaterally, the diplomatic window collapses.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Iran PGSA 'controlled maritime zone' formalizes Hormuz toll system — maritime law experts say it violates UNCLOS
outlets.summary: Iran's PGSA Hormuz control regime positions Iran as a transit authority, not a blockader — a legal framing designed to complicate arguments for forcible passage. The $2M permit fee and registration requirement for all vessels extends Iran's economic leverage over global shipping indefinitely.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran PGSA declares 'controlled maritime zone' at Hormuz — Iran coordinating passage of 26 vessels daily
outlets.summary: Iranian state media frames the PGSA 'controlled maritime zone' as a successful assertion of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz — presenting it as Iran winning de facto control of a critical global waterway without military escalation.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Pezeshkian confirms Iran reviewing latest US proposal; Pakistan mediator in Tehran for two-day visit
outlets.summary: Iran state news agency frames the Pakistan mediation positively — emphasizing Iranian demands for asset release and end to 'maritime piracy' while Baghaei confirms message exchanges with US are continuing.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: IRGC threatens war 'beyond the region'; Pezeshkian: 'Dialogue is not surrender'
outlets.summary: Iran International covers the dual Iranian messaging strategy: IRGC threatening global escalation while Pezeshkian frames negotiations as power-from-strength. The outlet notes Netanyahu has said Iran regime change is 'possible, not guaranteed' — a formulation Iran views as an existential threat.
